name: UN General Assembly 1945 (UNGA under UN Charter Chapter IV)
summary: The United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-UN-Charter-Chapter-IV framework established by the UN Charter — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating on formal one-state-one-vote universal-representation framework + most-democratic-UN-body framework (UNGA established 24 October 1945 by UN Charter Chapter IV Article 9 framework + UNGA First Session began 10 January 1946 in London + UNGA composition 193 UN member states framework — formal foundational UNGA framework establishing comprehensive UNGA organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate on different substantive areas, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating on formal one-state-one-vote universal-representation framework + most-democratic-UN-body framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal recommendatory-resolutions framework framework non-binding + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal six-Main-Committees framework: 1st Committee Disarmament + 2nd Economic and Financial + 3rd Social Humanitarian Cultural + 4th Special Political and Decolonization + 5th Administrative and Budgetary + 6th Legal framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Uniting for Peace Resolution 377 V 1950 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal UNGA Special Sessions framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Emergency Special Sessions ES framework via 11 ES held 1956-2024 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal MDGs 2000 + SDGs 2015 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Pact for the Future 2024 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan 1971 framework via UN General Assembly Resolution 2758 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Russia-aggression-against-Ukraine ES-11 2022-2024 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Israeli-occupation-Palestinian-territories ES-10 1997-2024 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Veto Initiative Resolution 76/262 2022 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Pact for the Future + Declaration on Future Generations 2024 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal UN80 Reform Process 2025-2026 framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal UNGA Uniting for Peace + Emergency Special Sessions framework (UNGA Resolution 377 V Uniting for Peace adopted 3 November 1950 + 11 Emergency Special Sessions ES held 1956-2024 framework: ES-1 Suez 1956 + ES-2 Hungary 1956 + ES-3 Lebanon-Jordan 1958 + ES-4 Congo 1960 + ES-5 Six-Day War 1967 + ES-6 Afghanistan 1980 + ES-7 Palestine 1980-1982 + ES-8 Namibia 1981 + ES-9 Golan Heights 1982 + ES-10 Israeli-Occupied-Palestinian-Territories 1997-2024 + ES-11 Russia-Aggression-Against-Ukraine 2022-2024 framework — formal foundational UNGA Uniting for Peace + Emergency Special Sessions framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Uniting for Peace + Emergency Special Sessions framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal UNSC-bypass-via-UNGA framework when UNSC paralysed by P5 veto framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal UNGA Six Main Committees framework (UNGA Six Main Committees providing formal six Main Committees framework: 1st + 2nd + 3rd + 4th + 5th + 6th Committee framework — formal foundational UNGA Six Main Committees framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal six Main Committees framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal Pact for the Future 2024 framework (Pact for the Future adopted 22 September 2024 at UN Summit of the Future New York + Declaration on Future Generations + Global Digital Compact + UNSC reform commitments framework — formal foundational Pact for the Future framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Pact for the Future framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Declaration on Future Generations + Global Digital Compact framework). The UNGA is operating upon 193 UN member states framework — comprehensive universal coverage framework. The Assembly is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal UNGA UNCRC + CEDAW + CRPD adoption framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal UNGA Resolution 44/25 of 20 November 1989 adopting UNCRC framework + UNGA Resolution 34/180 of 18 December 1979 adopting CEDAW framework + UNGA Resolution 61/106 of 13 December 2006 adopting CRPD framework. The UNGA President + UNGA Bureau + Six Main Committees + UNGA Plenary are the formal UNGA bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The UNGA is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: UN Charter Chapter IV Articles 9-22 (UNGA Framework)
statutory_framework.title: UN Charter Chapter IV Articles 9-22
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Charter Chapter IV Articles 9-22 framework on UNGA: composition + functions + powers + voting + procedure framework — formal foundational UNGA framework.

statutory_framework.title: UN Charter Article 18
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Charter Article 18 framework on UNGA voting: important questions 2/3 majority + others simple majority framework — formal foundational UNGA voting framework.

statutory_framework.citation: UNGA Resolution 377 V Uniting for Peace 1950
statutory_framework.title: UNGA Resolution 377 V Uniting for Peace
statutory_framework.relevance: UNGA Resolution 377 V Uniting for Peace adopted 3 November 1950 — formal foundational UNGA Uniting for Peace + Emergency Special Sessions framework.

statutory_framework.title: Pact for the Future
statutory_framework.relevance: Pact for the Future adopted 22 September 2024 at UN Summit of the Future New York — formal foundational Pact for the Future + Declaration on Future Generations + Global Digital Compact framework.

statutory_framework.title: UNGA Resolution 76/262 Veto Initiative
statutory_framework.relevance: UNGA Resolution 76/262 of 26 April 2022 — formal foundational Veto Initiative framework requiring UNGA debate on every veto.

apex_courts.name: UN General Assembly Plenary
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme deliberative-recommendatory body for UNGA — composed of 193 UN member states with one-state-one-vote framework.

apex_courts.name: UNGA President
apex_courts.role: Formal UNGA President body — elected by UNGA for 1-year term + rotates among 5 regional groups.

apex_courts.name: UNGA Bureau
apex_courts.role: Formal UNGA Bureau body composed of UNGA President + 21 Vice-Presidents + 6 Main Committee Chairs.

apex_courts.name: Six Main Committees
apex_courts.role: Formal six UNGA Main Committees: 1st Disarmament + 2nd Economic and Financial + 3rd Social Humanitarian Cultural + 4th Special Political and Decolonization + 5th Administrative and Budgetary + 6th Legal.

anonymisation_convention: UNGA-related communications operate via state-state framework via UNGA Plenary + Main Committees; individual communications not formally accepted under UNGA framework.

key_developments.title: UNGA established + UN Charter
key_developments.description: UN Charter signed 26 June 1945 in San Francisco + effective 24 October 1945 + UNGA established 24 October 1945 by UN Charter Chapter IV Article 9 — formal foundational UNGA framework operational.

key_developments.title: First UNGA Session
key_developments.description: First UNGA Session held 10 January - 14 February 1946 in London — formal first UNGA Session framework operational.

key_developments.title: UDHR adopted
key_developments.description: Universal Declaration of Human Rights UDHR adopted 10 December 1948 by UNGA Resolution 217 A III — formal foundational UDHR framework operational.

key_developments.title: Uniting for Peace + Korea + ES-1 Suez
key_developments.description: UNGA Resolution 377 V Uniting for Peace adopted 3 November 1950 — formal foundational UNGA Uniting for Peace framework operational.

key_developments.title: ES-1 Suez + ES-2 Hungary first Emergency Special Sessions
key_developments.description: ES-1 Suez Crisis 1-10 November 1956 + ES-2 Hungary 4-10 November 1956 — formal first Emergency Special Sessions framework operational.

key_developments.title: China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan
key_developments.description: UN General Assembly Resolution 2758 of 25 October 1971 — formal China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan framework operational.

key_developments.title: CEDAW adopted
key_developments.description: CEDAW adopted 18 December 1979 by UNGA Resolution 34/180 — formal foundational CEDAW framework operational.

key_developments.title: UNCRC adopted
key_developments.description: UNCRC adopted 20 November 1989 by UNGA Resolution 44/25 — formal foundational UNCRC framework operational.

key_developments.title: ES-10 Israeli-Occupied-Palestinian-Territories
key_developments.description: ES-10 Israeli-Occupied-Palestinian-Territories began 24-25 April 1997 + ongoing 1997-2024 framework — formal longest-running Emergency Special Session framework operational.

key_developments.title: MDGs adopted
key_developments.description: Millennium Development Goals MDGs adopted 8 September 2000 by UNGA Resolution 55/2 — formal foundational MDGs framework operational.

key_developments.title: Human Rights Council + CRPD adopted
key_developments.description: Human Rights Council established 15 March 2006 by UNGA Resolution 60/251 + CRPD adopted 13 December 2006 by UNGA Resolution 61/106 — formal foundational Human Rights Council + CRPD framework operational.

key_developments.title: SDGs adopted
key_developments.description: Sustainable Development Goals SDGs adopted 25 September 2015 by UNGA Resolution 70/1 — formal foundational SDGs framework operational.

key_developments.title: ES-11 Russia-Aggression-Against-Ukraine + Veto Initiative
key_developments.description: ES-11 Russia-Aggression-Against-Ukraine began 28 February 2022 + ES-11/1 Aggression Against Ukraine adopted 2 March 2022 + UNGA Resolution 76/262 Veto Initiative of 26 April 2022 + Russia-suspended-Human-Rights-Council 7 April 2022 by ES-11/3 framework — formal ES-11 + Veto Initiative + Russia-suspended-HRC framework operational.

key_developments.title: Pact for the Future + Summit of the Future + Declaration on Future Generations + Global Digital Compact
key_developments.description: Pact for the Future adopted 22 September 2024 at UN Summit of the Future New York + Declaration on Future Generations + Global Digital Compact framework — formal foundational Pact for the Future + Summit of the Future framework operational.

key_developments.title: UN80 Reform Process + 80th anniversary
key_developments.description: UN80 Reform Process 2025-2026 framework via UN Secretary-General Guterres-UN80-Initiative March 2025 + UN 80th anniversary 24 October 2025 framework — formal UN80 Reform + 80th anniversary framework operational.

structural_findings: UNGA operates a supranational global deliberative-recommendatory framework — places UNGA in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating on formal one-state-one-vote universal-representation framework + most-democratic-UN-body framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating on formal one-state-one-vote universal-representation framework + most-democratic-UN-body framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal recommendatory-resolutions framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal six-Main-Committees framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Uniting for Peace Resolution 377 V framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Emergency Special Sessions framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan 1971 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Russia-aggression-against-Ukraine ES-11 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Israeli-occupation-Palestinian-territories ES-10 framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Veto Initiative framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Pact for the Future framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal UN80 Reform Process framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal UNGA Uniting for Peace + Emergency Special Sessions framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal UNSC-bypass-via-UNGA framework when UNSC paralysed by P5 veto framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal UNGA Six Main Committees framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal six Main Committees framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Pact for the Future 2024 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Pact for the Future framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Declaration on Future Generations + Global Digital Compact framework.
193 UN member state ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive universal coverage framework.

1945 UN Charter + UNGA + 1946 first session + 1948 UDHR + 1950 Uniting for Peace + 1956 ES-1 + 1971 China-PRC + 1979 CEDAW + 1989 UNCRC + 1997 ES-10 Palestine + 2000 MDGs + 2006 HRC + CRPD + 2015 SDGs + 2022 ES-11 Russia + Veto Initiative + 2024 Pact for Future + 2025 UN80 UNGA-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

UNGA + UN Charter Chapter IV + Six Main Committees + Uniting for Peace + Emergency Special Sessions + Pact for the Future + UDHR + CEDAW + UNCRC + CRPD adoption formal decet framework with formal UNGA Resolution 44/25 UNCRC adoption + Resolution 34/180 CEDAW adoption + Resolution 61/106 CRPD adoption PA-intersection framework.
editorial_notes: UNGA jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global one-state-one-vote universal-representation foundational-UN-Charter-Chapter-IV meta-framework (UN Charter Chapter IV Articles 9-22 + Article 18 voting + Uniting for Peace Resolution 377 V 1950 + 11 Emergency Special Sessions 1956-2024 + Six Main Committees + Pact for the Future 2024 + Declaration on Future Generations + Global Digital Compact + UDHR Resolution 217 A III 1948 + CEDAW Resolution 34/180 1979 + UNCRC Resolution 44/25 1989 + CRPD Resolution 61/106 2006 + MDGs Resolution 55/2 2000 + SDGs Resolution 70/1 2015 + Veto Initiative Resolution 76/262 2022 + ES-10 Israeli-Occupied-Palestinian-Territories 1997-2024 + ES-11 Russia-Aggression-Against-Ukraine 2022-2024 + China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan Resolution 2758 1971 + Human Rights Council Resolution 60/251 2006 + Russia-suspended-HRC ES-11/3 2022 + 1945 UN Charter + UNGA + 1946 first session + 1948 UDHR + 1950 Uniting for Peace + 1956 ES-1 + 1971 China-PRC + 1979 CEDAW + 1989 UNCRC + 1997 ES-10 + 2000 MDGs + 2006 HRC + CRPD + 2015 SDGs + 2022 ES-11 + Veto + 2024 Pact + 2025 UN80). Only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating on formal one-state-one-vote universal-representation framework + most-democratic-UN-body framework + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal recommendatory-resolutions + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal six-Main-Committees + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal Uniting for Peace + Emergency Special Sessions UNSC-bypass-when-UNSC-paralysed-by-P5-veto + only modern global supranational deliberative-recommendatory framework operating formal 11 ES held 1956-2024: ES-1 Suez + ES-2 Hungary + ES-3 Lebanon-Jordan + ES-4 Congo + ES-5 Six-Day-War + ES-6 Afghanistan + ES-7 Palestine + ES-8 Namibia + ES-9 Golan-Heights + ES-10 Israeli-Occupied-Palestinian-Territories-1997-2024-longest-running + ES-11 Russia-Aggression-Against-Ukraine-2022-2024 + central jurisdiction of formal Pact for the Future + Declaration on Future Generations + Global Digital Compact + 193 UN member states + UNGA Headquartered New York + UNGA President rotates 5 regional groups + 1945 UNGA + 1946 first session + 1948 UDHR + 1950 Uniting for Peace + 1956 ES-1 + 1971 China-PRC + 1979 CEDAW + 1989 UNCRC + 1997 ES-10 Palestine + 2000 MDGs + 2006 HRC + CRPD + 2015 SDGs + 2022 ES-11 Russia + Veto + 2024 Pact + 2025 UN80 UNGA-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: UNGA UNCRC + CEDAW + CRPD adoption framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal UNGA Resolution 44/25 of 20 November 1989 adopting UNCRC framework + UNGA Resolution 34/180 of 18 December 1979 adopting CEDAW framework + UNGA Resolution 61/106 of 13 December 2006 adopting CRPD framework — UNGA is the formal adopter of the most-PA-relevant UN human rights treaties.
